layers.name: Core RF and Network Mathematics
layers.description: Frequency, network, circuit, transmission-line, vector-fitting, Q-factor, time-domain, and shared numerical abstractions that form scikit-rf's computational foundation.

layers.name: Calibration and De-embedding
layers.description: Calibration algorithms, calibration-set orchestration, and de-embedding models that correct measured microwave networks.

layers.name: Transmission Media Models
layers.description: Analytical models for coaxial, coplanar, microstrip, waveguide, free-space, distributed-circuit, and device transmission media.

layers.name: I/O, Reference Data, and RF Fixtures
layers.description: Touchstone, CITI, MDIF, CSV, metadata, packaged reference networks, and RF measurement fixtures consumed by the library, tests, and examples.

layers.name: Instrument Control
layers.description: SCPI validation and vendor-specific VNA drivers for HP, Keysight, NanoVNA, and Rohde and Schwarz instruments.

layers.name: Applications, Plotting, and Notebook UI
layers.description: Application package entry points, plotting helpers, Touchstone visualization, and notebook integrations that present RF results to users.

layers.name: Tests and Verification
layers.description: Pytest suites and test support code that verify core RF math, media, calibration, I/O, and instrument integrations.

layers.name: Documentation and Examples
layers.description: Sphinx sources, tutorials, notebooks, example assets, installation guidance, licensing, and API references for scikit-rf users and contributors.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand scikit-rf as an object-oriented toolkit for microwave and RF engineering. It establishes the measurement, network-analysis, and modeling problems that motivate every subsystem visited later in the tour.

tour.title: Public Package Surface
tour.description: The top-level package initializer is the central public surface and the highest-fan-in file in the graph. It connects the project overview to the major modules for networks, calibration, media, I/O, plotting, and instruments, so it is the best map of how users enter the library.
tour.languageLesson: A Python package initializer can re-export selected names from internal modules, giving users a concise public API while the implementation remains split across focused files.

tour.title: Numerical Foundations
tour.description: Frequency axes, physical constants, shared mathematical transformations, and utilities provide the vocabulary used by nearly every RF calculation. Their high fan-in makes them the right foundation before studying objects that store and manipulate measured network data.

tour.title: Network Domain Model
tour.description: Building on the numerical foundations, Network represents frequency-dependent multiport parameters, NetworkSet organizes related measurements, and Circuit composes arbitrary N-port topologies. These files form the core domain model and expose much of the behavior used by the specialized layers that follow.

tour.title: Calibration and Deembedding
tour.description: Calibration algorithms turn standards and raw measurements into corrected networks, while calibration sets coordinate repeated workflows. De-embedding then removes fixture and interconnect effects, applying the network model from the previous step to practical metrology problems.

tour.title: Transmission Media Models
tour.description: The Media abstraction converts physical transmission-line properties into networks that can be connected, calibrated, and analyzed with the core model. Concrete coplanar-waveguide, microstrip, and rectangular-waveguide implementations show how geometry, materials, and frequency-dependent propagation specialize that common interface.

tour.title: Measurement Data I/O
tour.description: The I/O layer bridges external RF data and the Network objects introduced earlier, with general serialization plus Touchstone, CITI, and MDIF parsers. The included PNA CSV table illustrates the real measurement fixtures used to keep these format boundaries grounded in instrument output.
tour.languageLesson: Files ending in .ts here are Touchstone 2.0 RF data fixtures, not TypeScript source; their keywords and numeric blocks describe network parameters and metadata.

tour.title: Instrument Control Boundary
tour.description: Virtual-instrument modules extend the file-oriented workflow into live VNA acquisition, with validators enforcing command and parameter constraints before hardware communication. Vendor-specific drivers such as the Keysight PNA and HP 8510C translate the common control surface into instrument dialects.

tour.title: Advanced RF Analysis
tour.description: Beyond basic network manipulation, these modules cover vector fitting, resonator Q-factor extraction, tapered lines, and time-domain transforms. They reuse the same frequency and network abstractions, demonstrating how the core model supports higher-level analysis without creating a separate data representation.

tour.title: Regression Test Strategy
tour.description: The tests exercise core networks, frequency handling, circuits, calibration, and parser behavior against both synthetic cases and RF fixtures. Reading them after the implementation layers reveals the expected numerical tolerances, edge cases, and compatibility contracts that a port must preserve.
tour.languageLesson: pytest organizes behavior as discoverable test functions and fixtures; parameterization is especially useful for applying the same numerical contract across formats, port counts, and frequency grids.

tour.title: Tutorials and Examples
tour.description: The Sphinx documentation index connects API references to curated tutorials and practical notebooks. The tutorial and example catalogs provide an application-level reading path that demonstrates how the core, calibration, media, I/O, and instrument pieces combine in real RF workflows.

tour.title: Build and Documentation
tour.description: Project metadata defines packaging, dependencies, and development tooling, while the Sphinx configuration and Makefile assemble the published documentation. Together they show how the Python modules and notebook-based learning material become an installable, maintainable library release.

tour.title: Continuous Validation
tour.description: GitHub Actions closes the tour by running the automated test, notebook, and linting checks that protect all preceding layers. These pipelines are the executable definition of supported environments and quality gates for contributions and release readiness.
tour.languageLesson: GitHub Actions YAML separates event triggers, parallel jobs, and sequential steps; matrices let one workflow verify multiple Python and operating-system combinations consistently.
